title: Environmental DNA Market Set to Surge to USD 5.61 Billion by 2031 with 10.2% CAGR
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global environmental DNA (eDNA) market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 10.2% from 2026 to 2031, driven by non-invasive species detection methods, stricter environmental regulations, and increasing demand for ecosystem health monitoring. North America leads the market with advanced research infrastructure and strong public-private collaborations, while air sampling and climate change impact assessment segments show particularly strong growth potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key company in the rapidly growing eDNA market with 10.2% CAGR, positioned to benefit from increased demand for advanced sequencing and molecular biology technologies.
Identified as a major player in the eDNA market, well-positioned to capitalize on growing demand for detection methods and testing solutions in environmental monitoring.
Key company in the market benefiting from technological advancements in sequencing tools and data analysis capabilities driving eDNA market expansion.
Listed as a leading service provider in the eDNA ecosystem, positioned to benefit from increased testing demand and scaling of laboratory capabilities.

title: Molecular Biology Enzymes, Reagents and Kits Market Size Worth USD 70.85 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global molecular biology enzymes, reagents and kits market is projected to grow from USD 27.69 billion in 2025 to USD 70.85 billion by 2035, with a CAGR of 9.68%. Growth is driven by increasing adoption of precision medicine, molecular diagnostics, genomics research, and biotechnology R&D. The US market is expected to reach $23.54 billion and Europe $19.32 billion by 2035, with Asia-Pacific showing the fastest growth.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a leading market player in the growing molecular biology enzymes, reagents and kits market, which is projected to reach $70.85 billion by 2035 with a 9.68% CAGR.

title: Global Single-use Bioreactors Market Forecast to Surge from USD 3.61 Billion in 2025 to USD 15.11 Billion by 2034
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global single-use bioreactors market is projected to experience robust growth with a CAGR of 17.27%, expanding from USD 3.61 billion in 2025 to USD 15.11 billion by 2034. Growth is driven by increasing biopharmaceutical demand, cost efficiency advantages, and technological advancements in bioprocessing. North America is expected to dominate, while Asia-Pacific shows rapid expansion. Major players include Sartorius, Thermo Fisher Scientific, and Cytiva, with recent developments including new product launches and facility expansions.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Identified as a major market player with recent product innovation (5 L DynaDrive system) and strategic facility expansions (AGC Biologics integration of 5,000 L reactors), demonstrating active market participation and growth.
Identified as a dominant market player focusing on expanding product offerings and technological capabilities in the growing single-use bioreactors sector.
Listed as a leading company in the single-use bioreactors market with strong market position and focus on expanding product offerings and technological capabilities.

title: Unlocking Growth in the Sweden Flu Market: The Rise of Molecular Diagnostics and Targeted Antiviral Therapies
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: Sweden's flu diagnostic and treatment market is projected to grow from USD 172.1 million in 2026 to USD 210.3 million by 2031 at a 4.1% CAGR, driven by high influenza burden among elderly populations and increased adoption of molecular diagnostics and targeted antiviral therapies. The market operates within Sweden's centralized healthcare system with key players including Roche, Thermo Fisher Scientific, 3M, and Meridian Bioscience, though growth is constrained by seasonality, regulatory compliance requirements, and supply chain vulnerabilities.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Roche holds a key position in both diagnostics and therapeutics segments of the Sweden flu market, with strong capabilities and established supply networks positioning it well for market growth.
Thermo Fisher excels in molecular platforms which are increasingly favored in core clinical settings for their sensitivity and capability to distinguish subtypes, aligning with market growth drivers.
3M provides infection prevention solutions in the competitive landscape and is positioned to benefit from the growing demand for diagnostic and treatment solutions in Sweden's flu market.

title: Global Pharmaceutical Filtration Market to Double by 2034: Insights on Driving Factors
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global pharmaceutical filtration market is expected to grow at a CAGR of 8.13% from 2025 to 2034, driven by rising biopharmaceutical demand, CDMO expansion, and increased sterile drug manufacturing. North America leads the market, while Asia-Pacific emerges as a rapidly growing region. Major players are investing in capacity expansion and acquisitions to meet growing demand, though high operational costs and filter clogging challenges persist.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Key market player (MilliporeSigma) in a rapidly expanding pharmaceutical filtration sector driven by biopharmaceutical demand and regulatory compliance needs.
Recently acquired Solventum's purification and filtration business for $4.1B in 2025, significantly strengthening bioprocess filtration capabilities and market position.
Listed as a key market player in the growing pharmaceutical filtration sector with opportunities in expanding biopharmaceutical production.
Listed as a leading company in the growing pharmaceutical filtration market with strong positioning to benefit from 8.13% CAGR expansion through 2034.
